title: asclepias breviantherae subsp. breviantherae
intro: Asclepias breviantherae subsp. breviantherae, also known as the short-anthered milkweed or short-anthered antelope-horn milkweed, is a species of milkweed native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the production of breast milk in lactating women and to treat various skin conditions, including eczema and acne. The health benefits of Asclepias breviantherae subsp. breviantherae include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe irritated skin, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
